description: A TOCTOU (Time-of-Check-Time-of-Use) race condition in the V8 RegExp parser allows a sandbox escape via out-of-bounds access on the RegExpCapture list. When parsing a regex containing backreferences or named captures, RegExpParserImpl::ScanForCaptures() eagerly scans the full pattern to determine the total capture count (stored in capture_count_), which is then used by GetCapture() to size the captures_ ZoneList. However, since the regex pattern string resides in sandbox-controlled memory, a concurrent worker thread can modify the pattern between the scan phase and the actual parse phase. If the scan phase observes N captures and the subsequent parse phase encounters M > N captures, GetCapture() allocates a list of length N but is then called with an index up to M, resulting in an out-of-bounds access beyond the ZoneList. The returned out-of-bounds RegExpCapture* pointer is then used in two exploit primitives: (1) capture->set_body(body) writes an attacker-controlled value to an arbitrary address, and (2) builder->AddAtom(body) performs an indirect call through the attacker-controlled pointer, enabling control flow hijack.

fixes.evidence_text: 6c5f6bdc3652c1bed3b5f3e4a42090cd684e4d75 [regexp] Harden access of captures during RegExp parsing
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6239220 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
